title: hort-server values schema
description: Install-time validation for the hort-server chart. Enforces strict-schema lint: `additionalProperties: false` on the top-level object and EVERY nested object block the chart owns the shape of, so a mistyped / retired / unknown key (e.g. the pre-078 top-level `apiBindAddr`, `worker.scanner.osvScanner`, or a typo like `worker.scanner.osvv`) FAILS `helm install`/`helm template` instead of being silently accepted and ignored. Free-form passthrough blocks the operator owns the shape of (`resources`, `probes.*`, `affinity`, `nodeSelector`, `*SecurityContext`, `gitopsConfig`, and the verbatim k8s array entries) intentionally stay permissive — the strict lint is for the chart's OWN config keys, not for arbitrary Kubernetes pod-spec content.

provider.description: Rule 2: enum oidc | disabled. The `basic` provider is retired; values from a prior chart release with provider=basic must migrate to provider=disabled + nativeTokens.enabled=true.

dex.description: Optional minimal OIDC IdP sidecar (de-admin reset, spec §3). Default off. When enabled, renders a Dex Deployment+Service+ConfigMap and points HORT_OIDC_ISSUER_URL at dex.issuerUrl.

allOf.description: If auth.dex.enabled, provider must be oidc, dex.issuerUrl must be non-empty, and the static-admin password-hash Secret must be set. The chart points HORT_OIDC_ISSUER_URL at Dex and Dex needs its admin hash; surface the misconfig at install time.
Rule 2 cont'd: if provider == oidc, oidc.issuerUrl + oidc.audience must be non-empty.
If tokenExchange.enabled, provider must be oidc AND cliClientId must be non-empty. Mirrors the binary's boot-time fail-closed check at the chart level so operators get a clear error before deploy.

If tokenExchange.enabled, nativeTokens.enabled MUST be true and signingKey.existingSecret MUST be non-empty. The exchange endpoint mints hort_cli_* (PAT-shape) tokens; the validator that recognises them only wires when native tokens are on. The binary boot-fails on this misconfig (ConfigError::TokenExchangeRequiresNativeTokens); the chart-level rule surfaces the error at install time instead of at server-startup time.

allowHttp.description: Opt in to plain HTTP S3 endpoints. Required for in-cluster Garage/MinIO without TLS. The binary's validator rejects this flag on https:// endpoints and on real AWS S3.

sseMode.description: Server-side-encryption mode. "" (unset) sends no opinion and falls back to bucket-default. "bucket-default" suppresses the non-AWS WARN. "sse256" requests SSE-S3 (AES256). "sse-kms" requests SSE-KMS and requires sseKmsKeyArn.

sseKmsKeyArn.description: Full KMS key ARN. Required when sseMode == "sse-kms"; ignored otherwise.

allOf.description: Rule 3 cont'd: if backend == s3, s3.endpoint + s3.bucket + s3.existingSecret non-empty.

evictableUrl.description: HORT_REDIS_URL_EVICTABLE override for the evictable class. Empty → falls back to redis.url. Mutually exclusive with evictableExistingSecret (Rule 4b).

evictableExistingSecret.description: K8s Secret name for the evictable-class URL. Mutually exclusive with evictableUrl (Rule 4b).

evictableSecretKey.description: Key inside evictableExistingSecret holding the HORT_REDIS_URL_EVICTABLE value.

durableUrl.description: HORT_REDIS_URL_DURABLE override for the durable class. Empty → falls back to redis.url. Mutually exclusive with durableExistingSecret (Rule 4c).

durableExistingSecret.description: K8s Secret name for the durable-class URL. Mutually exclusive with durableUrl (Rule 4c).

durableSecretKey.description: Key inside durableExistingSecret holding the HORT_REDIS_URL_DURABLE value.

allOf.description: Rule 4 cont'd: if backend == redis, redis.url OR redis.existingSecret non-empty.

Rule 4b: per-class evictable exclusivity — when at least one of redis.evictableUrl / redis.evictableExistingSecret is non-empty, exactly one of the two must be non-empty. Operators who do not use the per-class override pay no constraint.
Rule 4c: per-class durable exclusivity — when at least one of redis.durableUrl / redis.durableExistingSecret is non-empty, exactly one of the two must be non-empty.

Rule 4d: broken-fallback install-block — when backend == redis AND main redis.url AND redis.existingSecret are BOTH empty AND any per-class override (evictableUrl / evictableExistingSecret / durableUrl / durableExistingSecret) is non-empty, the absent class would have no URL to fall back to. Reject at chart-render time with a clear error rather than at runtime.

redis.description: ephemeralStore.redis: per-class override set but the main fallback (redis.url or redis.existingSecret) is empty — the absent class would have no URL to fall back to. Set redis.url or redis.existingSecret, OR set both per-class overrides explicitly.

image.description: Container image for the hort-server binary. Image-signature enforcement is the operator's policy controller's job (`cosign.publicKey` is the PEM the controller verifies against; the chart does not enforce it).

api.description: Main API listener. `bindAddr` (HORT_API_BIND) follows the `<subsystem>.bindAddr` shape shared by `metrics.bindAddr` and `control.bindAddr`; the top-level `apiBindAddr` key is retired (HARD rename, no alias).

bindAddr.description: Bind address for the main API listener (HORT_API_BIND). Non-empty string; e.g. '0.0.0.0:8080' (default), '127.0.0.1:8080', or a non-default port like '0.0.0.0:9080'.

publishBodyMaxSize.description: HORT_PUBLISH_BODY_MAX_SIZE — per-publish body-size ceiling as a human-readable size string (e.g. "300Mi", "1Gi", or a bare byte integer). Empty leaves the env var unset (binary default, 300 MiB); an explicit "0" is the refuse-all-publishes kill-switch.

upstream.description: Storage backstops on the streaming upstream fetch path. Human-readable size strings (e.g. "64Mi", "1Gi", "64M", or a bare byte integer); the binary enforces a 1024-byte floor.

userAgent.description: HORT_UPSTREAM_USER_AGENT — outbound User-Agent for upstream pull-through fetches (hort-server + worker). Empty → the binary built-in hort/<version>; crates.io requires a non-empty UA. Must be a valid HTTP header value: no control characters (the pattern rejects C0 controls except TAB, plus DEL — matching what the binary accepts); the same value is offline-validatable via `hort-server validate-config`.

control.description: Internal-only control-plane listener (HORT_CONTROL_BIND). Empty bindAddr ⇒ control routes stay on the main listener.

oci.description: OCI Distribution surfaces. The OCI upload timeout (HORT_HTTP_OCI_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS) is exposed here as `uploadTimeoutSeconds`; the prior `http.ociUploadTimeoutSeconds` key is retired (HARD rename, no alias).

uploadTimeoutSeconds.description: HORT_HTTP_OCI_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS — OCI long-tail upload window in seconds. Default 3600 (60 min).

legacyCatalogEnabled.description: HORT_OCI_LEGACY_CATALOG_ENABLED — opt-in to the aggregating /v2/_catalog endpoint. Default false.

maxSessionsPerPrincipal.description: HORT_OCI_MAX_SESSIONS_PER_PRINCIPAL — max concurrent OCI upload sessions per principal. 0 ⇒ binary default (32).

extraCaBundle.description: Additive HORT_EXTRA_CA_BUNDLE trust anchors. `path` is the in-container mount path. `configMapName` (Recipe A) and `secretName` (Recipe B) are mutually-exclusive auto-mount sources holding the PEM under key `ca.crt`; either one auto-mounts the bundle symmetrically on the server + worker + CronJobs and wires the env. Neither set = fully-manual extraVolumes recipe (chart mounts nothing and sets no env).

allOf.description: configMapName and secretName are mutually exclusive — a pod can mount only one ca.crt at `path`. When at least one is non-empty, exactly one must be.
An auto-mount source requires a non-empty `path` (the mount path + the env value).

scheduledTasks.description: Unified scheduled-task tree. ALL periodic tasks live here regardless of execution path; each carries an `executionPath` attribute (dsn-direct | admin-task). `executionPath: dsn-direct` tasks run a `hort-server` subcommand with the runtime DSN only (no svc-token), gated solely by their own `enabled`. `executionPath: admin-task` tasks invoke `hort-cli admin task invoke <kind>` against the admin-task HTTP endpoint with the `<release>-svc-token` PAT, gated by BOTH `adminTasksEnabled` (the master toggle, which also gates the svc-token-bootstrap Job + RBAC) AND their own `enabled`. Replaces the prior `cronJobs.*` block + the five top-level task keys.

adminTasksEnabled.description: Global master toggle for the `executionPath: admin-task` tasks + the service-account token bootstrap Job (+ its RBAC). Replaces the pre-078 `cronJobs.enabled`. Default: disabled. Does NOT gate the `executionPath: dsn-direct` tasks (those keep their own per-task `enabled`).

svcTokenKubectlImage.description: kubectl image used by the svc-token-bootstrap Job's write-secret container. Replaces the pre-078 `cronJobs.kubectlImage`.

rotateSvcToken.description: When true, force-rotate the cronjob-tasks svc-token on the next helm upgrade. Replaces the pre-078 `cronJobs.rotateSvcToken`. Default false.

scrub.description: executionPath: dsn-direct. CAS integrity scrub CronJob. `actionOnMismatch` is also read by the main Deployment, so it is load-bearing even when disabled.

quarantineReleaseSweep.description: executionPath: dsn-direct. Periodic quarantine release-sweep CronJob. Inserts a `kind='quarantine-release-sweep'` row every tick using the runtime DSN; the worker dispatches to `QuarantineReleaseSweepHandler`, which feeds the candidacy query into the fail-closed `release_expired` check. Deliberately does NOT use the `adminTasksEnabled` umbrella or `svc-token-bootstrap` chain.

prefetchTick.description: executionPath: dsn-direct. Periodic prefetch scheduled-tick CronJob. Inserts a `kind='prefetch-tick'` row every tick using the runtime DSN; the worker dispatches to `PrefetchTickHandler`, which walks every Scheduled-trigger repo + tracked-package pair, fetches the upstream catalog via `UpstreamProxy`, extracts the published version set via `FormatHandler::extract_upstream_versions`, and invokes the planner with the real upstream-vs-held divergence. Default-disabled (prefetch is opt-in per-repo via `Repository.prefetch_policy`). Deliberately does NOT use the `adminTasksEnabled` umbrella or `svc-token-bootstrap` chain.

prefetchRowRetentionSweep.description: executionPath: dsn-direct. Periodic prefetch-row retention-sweep CronJob. Inserts a `kind='prefetch-row-retention-sweep'` row every tick using the runtime DSN; the worker dispatches to `PrefetchRowRetentionSweepHandler`, which deletes terminal (`status IN ('completed', 'failed')`) `kind LIKE 'prefetch%'` rows older than a configurable horizon (default 7 days). Default-disabled (the cascade is opt-in per-repo via `prefetch_policy.triggers.contains(TransitiveDeps)`; if no repo opts in the table accumulates nothing). DSN-only delivery contract — no svc-token, no `adminTasksEnabled` umbrella.

wheelMetadataBackfill.description: executionPath: dsn-direct. Periodic wheel-metadata-backfill CronJob. Inserts a `kind='wheel-metadata-backfill'` row every tick using the runtime DSN; the worker dispatches to `WheelMetadataBackfillHandler`, which walks PyPI wheel artifacts whose `content_references` row of kind `wheel_metadata` is absent, streams each wheel from CAS, invokes `FormatHandler::extract_wheel_metadata_bytes`, and on `Some(bytes)` persists the bytes to CAS + inserts the ContentReference row — retroactively enabling PEP 658 advertisement for pre-existing wheels. Default-disabled (one-shot retrofit for operators upgrading from an earlier release; a freshly-deployed operator's wheel inventory is empty and the tick is a no-op). DSN-only delivery contract — no svc-token, no `adminTasksEnabled` umbrella.

batchSize.description: How many wheel artifacts the worker walks per invocation. The handler caps at 1000 regardless of input; values above 1000 silently clamp. Omit (or set 0) to use the handler default (100).

stagingSweep.description: executionPath: admin-task. Staging-area upload expiry sweep.

serviceAccountRotation.description: executionPath: admin-task. Service-account fallback PAT rotation CronJob. The `enabled` flag is the SINGLE source-of-truth toggle for fallback PAT rotation — it drives BOTH this CronJob AND the worker-side wiring (rotation env in worker-deployment.yaml + per-namespace RBAC). Worker-side parameters live under `worker.rotation.*` (targetNamespaces, publicRegistryHost). The top-level allOf rules require `adminTasksEnabled`, `worker.enabled`, and a non-empty `worker.rotation.publicRegistryHost` whenever this is true (fail-fast, no silent half-on).

eventstoreCheckpoint.description: executionPath: admin-task. Event-chain checkpoint emission CronJob. The worker side ALSO needs S3 storage + HORT_EVENT_CHAIN_ANCHOR_SIGNING_KEY_FILE / HORT_EVENT_CHAIN_ANCHOR_PUBKEY_FILE provisioned, and the anchor bucket created with S3 Object Lock + a COMPLIANCE-mode default retention.

replaySeenPrune.description: executionPath: admin-task. Replay-guard seen-set TTL cleanup — periodic DELETE FROM jwt_replay_seen WHERE expires_at < now(). DEFAULT-ENABLED: the only admin-task CronJob template that defaults to enabled, gated only by the `adminTasksEnabled` master toggle. No extra worker-side provisioning is needed. Cleanup degrades safe — disabling it only grows the table, replay protection never degrades.

scannerRegistryPrune.description: executionPath: admin-task. Scanner-worker registry housekeeping — periodic DELETE FROM scanner_registry WHERE last_heartbeat < now() - 7d. DEFAULT-ENABLED (like replaySeenPrune): steady-state cleanup of rows left by retired worker pods, gated only by the `adminTasksEnabled` master toggle. No extra worker-side provisioning is needed (it reuses the heartbeat's scanner_registry handle). Cleanup degrades safe — disabling it only grows the table; worker liveness is recomputed from last_heartbeat on every admin-list read, independent of pruning.

verifyEventChain.description: executionPath: admin-task (gating only — it actually runs the `hort-server` binary directly with the runtime DSN, no svc-token PAT). Event-chain tamper-evidence verifier CronJob. Runs `hort-server verify-event-chain --format json` DIRECTLY (an in-process reader using the runtime DML DSN + anchor object store, NOT a worker-dispatched task). Records a `kind='verify-event-chain'` liveness breadcrumb on each completed run; the boot-time `hort_event_chain_verify_overdue` gauge reads the newest one. DEFAULT-DISABLED. Keep the schedule consistent with HORT_EVENT_CHAIN_VERIFY_EXPECTED_INTERVAL_SECS (default 86400 s = daily).

retentionEvaluate.description: executionPath: admin-task. Event-sourced retention-evaluate sweep: read active retention policies + keyset-paginated non-protected artifacts, append one ArtifactExpired per fresh match (deletes nothing). DEFAULT-DISABLED (retention is operator-opt-in). Enable this first, observe, then retentionPurge.

retentionPurge.description: executionPath: admin-task. Destructive storage-GC walk: for every ArtifactExpired with no following ArtifactPurged, decrement content_references, delete the CAS blob iff the cross-kind refcount hit 0, append ArtifactPurged. Refuses to run until the worker boot refcount-reconcile sweep converged. DEFAULT-DISABLED — the most destructive retention task.

eventstoreArchive.description: executionPath: admin-task. Audit-retention stream sweep: seal terminal / age-gated streams past the configured retention floor via the StreamSealed-tombstone chokepoint. Actual row removal needs the worker HORT_RETENTION_DATABASE_URL (hort_retention_role); unset → fail-safe no-op via the events_immutable trigger. DEFAULT-DISABLED. Weekly cadence.

worker.description: Multi-kind worker Deployment (Trivy + osv-scanner + cron-rescan-tick + advisory-watch-tick + staging-sweep + noop). Default disabled; operators opt in by setting `enabled: true`.

provenance.description: Sigstore/cosign provenance verification. `cosign.enabled` is LOAD-BEARING (gates ProvenancePort + provenance-verify handler registration, NOT cosmetic). When true the verifier loads a PINNED trusted_root.json from `cosign.trustedRootFile` at boot (the verify path is offline — no live TUF/Rekor/Fulcio fetch) and the worker refuses to boot if that file is missing.

metrics.description: Worker /metrics Prometheus scrape endpoint. `enabled: true` sets HORT_WORKER_METRICS_BIND=0.0.0.0:<port>, exposes the container port, and (when networkPolicy.enabled) co-renders a worker NetworkPolicy adding a Prometheus-scoped Ingress allowance on <port>. The listener has NO per-request auth — the NetworkPolicy is the access control. The default-on server NetworkPolicy already DENIES this port; this knob adds the scrape allowance. `scrapeFrom` is a list of verbatim k8s NetworkPolicyPeer objects (the ingress `from[]`) and is REQUIRED (minItems 1) when enabled:true: declaring who may scrape is mandatory, because an empty `from` would mean ALL sources (fail-OPEN) per the k8s NetworkPolicy spec. The template renders `ingress: []` (genuine deny) when no scrapers are declared, but the schema rejects enabling the listener without them.

db.description: Worker Postgres pool knobs. The `lockTimeoutMs` defense-in-depth backstop is applied (via .after_connect) to BOTH worker pools (main + the optional hort_retention_role retention pool) and bounds only lock-acquisition wait, so it fires on the pathological 'blocked on a peer's uncommitted unique slot' case and never aborts a legitimately slow large-stream DELETE. It exists because eventstore-archive's seal_and_remove appends a StreamSealed tombstone with no internal wait bound — safe only under the single-flight precondition (CronJob concurrencyPolicy: Forbid + worker per-kind semaphore + per-UTC-day idempotency key).

lockTimeoutMs.description: HORT_WORKER_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS — Postgres lock_timeout (milliseconds) for both worker pools. Default 120000 (2 min), generous on purpose: under the single-flight invariant it must never fire in correct operation; a small value risks a false abort if single-flight is ever deliberately relaxed. 0 disables the backstop — only set 0 if you have an alternative single-flight enforcement; doing so re-opens the unbounded-block failure mode this backstop covers.

rotation.description: Service-account PAT rotation worker-side PARAMETERS. These are NOT an on/off switch: rotation is enabled by the single source-of-truth toggle `scheduledTasks.serviceAccountRotation.enabled`, which drives both the CronJob and the worker-side wiring. `targetNamespaces` / `publicRegistryHost` here only configure what the worker writes. The cross-field couplings that must hold when rotation is on (umbrella, worker enabled, non-empty publicRegistryHost) are enforced by the top-level `allOf` rules.

items.description: k8s namespace name — DNS-1123 label syntax (lowercase alphanumerics + hyphens, must start and end with an alphanumeric, max 63 chars).

allOf.description: Rule 8a: if replicaCount > 1, ephemeralStore.backend MUST be redis (multi-node requires Redis for shared ephemeral state).
Rule 8b: if replicaCount > 1, storage.backend MUST be s3 (RWO PVC cannot multi-attach).

Rule 9a: fallback PAT rotation is enabled by the SINGLE toggle `scheduledTasks.serviceAccountRotation.enabled`. When it is true, the `scheduledTasks.adminTasksEnabled` master umbrella MUST also be true — the rotation CronJob is an `executionPath: admin-task` template that renders only under the umbrella, so without it the single toggle would silently render no CronJob (a half-on no-op). Mirrors the `replicaCount ⇒ ephemeralStore/storage` top-level couplings: fail fast at `helm install` rather than half-enabling at runtime.

Rule 9b: when `scheduledTasks.serviceAccountRotation.enabled` is true, `worker.enabled` MUST be true AND `worker.rotation.publicRegistryHost` MUST be non-empty. The single rotation toggle drives the worker-side wiring (the rotation env block in worker-deployment.yaml + the per-namespace RBAC), which is meaningless without the worker Deployment and has nowhere to point the `dockerconfigjson.auths` map without the registry host. Fail fast at install rather than producing a worker that cannot rotate. Mirrors the `replicaCount ⇒ …` couplings' shape.
